item.title: Staff Post - Open
item.content:encoded: To reduce the amount of articles which contain NC images, the image used in this article has been removed.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I like this one. I'm guessing normal usage of this is to ensure people stay quiet during theater performances and the like, and I kinda want that. Only it would be best for strict dramas or tragedies rather than comedies.

item.title: Staff Post - Open
item.content:encoded:
Timer Expired. Edits made as discussed below. Author may revert at any time.

The WikiWalk team is proposing edits to this page. All proposed edits will be in a collapsible below this post, and after 7 days if there are no serious objections to the edits, the feedback received will be taken into account as the edits are made. If you have questions not directly related to the article about this project, please pm a member of the WikiWalk Team

+ Show Block
- Hide Block

The tale series Parable of the Wayward Prince can be called the closest thing the Alexylva University GoI has to a hub page, and serves a great place for anyone interested in reading about AU skips and tales. Just as with SCP-1080 before it, linking this skip there would help facilitate such a leg of the wikiwalk, As such, I am recommending the following edits:

Changing this:

Alexylva University's Merry Integrators Player Company welcomes you to a [show, performance, farce]! Please refrain from [thought-provoking? talking, expressing] or otherwise disturbing the players for the duration of the performance. Thank you for your [cooperation, obedience].

To this:

Alexylva University's Merry Integrators Player Company welcomes you to a [show, performance, farce]! Please refrain from [thought-provoking? talking, expressing] or otherwise disturbing the players for the duration of the performance. Thank you for your [cooperation, obedience].

If anyone has other/better examples that could be used here please leave a comment saying so!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Absolutely agree with you here. I suffer from something similar to this sort of aphasia on occasions (I think it's the way my personality disorder interacts with certain drugs) and it can be truly frightening/depressing when it occurs. When it restricts you to being a viewer and never a participant, I think it sort of magnifies this profound sense of incommunicable loneliness and isolation I feel is inherent to human consciousness.

Nonetheless, I gotta say the pic almost made me drop it right away. The pair of humanoid speakers is kind of a strange visual but in, like, a very cheap way, so for me the first impression they gave was pretty bad and kinda stayed with me while I was reading it… it's just distracting…

On the flipside I really like your tech/culture explanation and think it's a concept well worth expanding on (preferably by referencing past observations of the adverse effects caused by testing or by their accidental misuse)

item.title: Image Attribution
item.content:encoded: The image in this article is a CC BY-NC-SA image from this site. Note that the image is used here under a different license than the rest of the site. Commercial use of the image is not permited by its creator.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
*applauds entirety of comment* I concur, all of this should be taken into account (not that I would have thought of any of it on my own). You also have a very refreshing sense of humor, I like it. XD

(^Is basically a really long way of saying "This.".)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
First off, +1. Applause. I have been reading the University SCPs in near-reverse-chronological order. Knowing this is the piece in which was first named makes it really cool.

Personally, love the concept. Anyone who has worked as a teacher should have a(n evil) desire to own one of these. Yes, it is a "mundane" item, which is why it doesn't carry as much of a hook. It is an SCP due to it's ability to deactivate a specific portion of a human brain AND the fact that it draws power from nearby systems. I see this as one of those ethical corners the University seems to like cutting: literally putting responsibility for a student's mind in the teacher's hand.

One quibble: Power

Foundation researchers were able to build an adapter to power the device at █.█ V and ██ A.

First, AC or DC? DC would be assumed based on its use in similar products in our 'Verse, but there are also AC/AC transformers. So fairly low voltage, but high amperage; that much current would require somewhat heavy wires to transmit at that low of a voltage without melting.1 (ex. my notebook runs at 19.5V at 4.62A). Most small consumer electronics usually run at 9-12VDC @ 500ma (.5a). Automotive electrical systems use 12-14V @ 20-40A under the hood. Assuming this device uses ambient energy to power it, I would think less current would be required. Either that, or it's got one HELL of an amplifier in there (those prefer 110VAC @ 5-10A).

Second, the device is capable of drawing enough power for the Shutdown/Resume from ambient/nearby power systems. I can see the University using this system for everything. Fixed power-loops in the walls that devices can draw from. WiPow? Cloud Power? Be damned useful in a classroom environment if everyone's laptop was able to charge without anyone tripping over adapter cords. But if it can do this for the mind-altering settings, why does it need an external adapter or have a port for one at all?

I would recommend adding a Faraday Cage to the containment procedure. Using one around the device when used should keep a lot of the ambient energy from being able to get to the device, reducing the draw on our facilities. The Foundation should be at no real risk if it is actually off when switched to "Inactive".

I am assuming that any experimentation with the other functions have been left out to keep this article at a reasonable length. Unfortunately, I am left wondering what "Density" and "Rapidity" do. Does this drill the lecture itself into the students' skulls? Maybe a collapsible (sorry for suggesting it) experiment log?

Whee! That was a ramble…

For some strange reason I can see the administration of the university being indirectly responsible for the universe of the Paranoia RPG…but I can be described as 'odd' on an off Thursday or Friday. Nothing to see here, move along.

EDIT: This item has only one presentation/performance/recording. Are there any others? How is the content up/downloaded? Am I asking way too many questions because I'm wired on coffee? "Please step away from the keyboard, sir. You need to swallow this, Doctor. Yes, it is a class C Amnesiac. Please do not make me shoot you, sir. Thank you."

Footnotes
1. Anyone with a stronger knowledge of electricity is invited to correct.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Upvoted for the recovery report.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
The idea is (though I suppose this isn't mentioned directly) that it's affecting your ability to communicate with the outside world entirely, which is typically limited to speech. I should mention sign language. It was inspired by knowing the personal frustration of having a speech disorder, and the frustration that goes along with having an abundance of ideas and thoughts and having no assurance that you'll be able to express them when the time comes. Because the words don't work for you, and you know the words, but they don't come out. You have things to say, but you can't say them.

But sign language is a point.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Moreover, affected subjects after this point will develop deep clinical depression and will frequently attempt suicide within the week; this is believed to be a standard reaction to such a sudden change in one's ability to communicate, rather than a secondary effect.

Have they tested this with someone fluent in ASL who has deaf friends? Not speaking would still suck, but still being able to communicate easily with some people could help.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I don't particularly care for this at all. The Recovery Report and University stuff I like, and that addendum is the reason I'm not downvoting, but the object itself I find uninteresting.

(Also, I assumed that the University goes under multiple names, so that part didn't bother me.)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: No vote from me. It isn't bad in any way that could earn a downvote, but it just doesn't click well enough to earn an upvote. I like the fleshing-out of the University (though I don't like that you gave it a name), but the object itself is just nondescript.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: For those who don't get why the effect is important: maybe it's just me, but when I tried to imagine permanently losing the ability to speak, in spite of wanting to (I have a speech disorder, so it's not completely foreign to me), I couldn't help but imagine losing my mind. It makes an odd sort of sense (to me) that a culture capable of having this sort of technology would use it like this, knowing and accepting it to be harmless when used properly. But when exposed to a very different culture without that sort of technology, it could be dangerous.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Undecided how to vote as yet but where can I get those speakers?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The suicide bit is stated to be consistent with people who lose ability to communicate through mundane means (i think, stroke or so) I am not a medic and have no idea if it is true, but it seems believable enough to not feel tacked on, at least to me.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: It's weird, I kind of liked the University better when it didn't have a name, still my favorite Group of Interest though, and seems like one that could, if given a separate wiki like Serpent's Hand or GOC, become as fleshed out as the Foundation it's self. still i enjoyed this quite a bit as is, with the changes suggested, it'll be incredible.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Meh. A lot of this article (like the part about suicide or the classification) felt tacked on and out of place. A little more detail (descriptions of the "performance?") would be nice. It seems to me like an okay idea meshed with random scaaaary effects.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I like the note but not much else, honestly…

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I don't get it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: At some point in the writing I started using "switch" and "dial" interchangeably. Thanks for catching.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: That too. Especially since those are the anomalous features, which would presumably require a more stable power source and be less desirable to have activate unintentionally. Even the University-verse should care somewhat about safety in its design.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Made an edit. It's not the third switch if it's the only switch.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I don't know about not using it at all, but I agree a "power leech" feature is useless if only 1 or 2 functions use it, rather than the whole device.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Why on earth would they build that feature in in addition to power cable? The ability to leech power from any system is one you actively wish to avoid.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
And now University has a name.

Speakers themselves are not very interesting, and I doubt I would've upvoted them as separate, not University related, SCP. Everything related to other timeline, including location names and the last note, are perfect. I suppose speakers, the way they are now, make sense from the viewpoint of people from the University who was planing to use them. But they still not interesting. Maybe there is another way something like this can be used? Forcing students to study by making them unable to think of anything else except what is currently broadcasted, I don't know. It's your call in the end, and I'll leave my upvote anyway just for the University characterization bits. I just wish they were a bit more… more by themselves.

I don't know why, but I like "Euclid classification is based on the potential ability to deplete Foundation power reserves if left unchecked." part very much.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Not unpowered, just not being fed power intentionally. It's drawing power from the room around it.

Yeah, I might change that. It gets weirder the more I say it aloud.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Nice fleshing out of the University universe, but I object to the item being able to produce its anomalous effects unpowered. That just seems like a cheap trick to make it more dangerous. It just doesn't make any sense, and seems entirely unnecessary. Also, this item should definitely be safe, since it doesn't do anything on its own, and even when it does do something, it's trivial to reverse.
